sql_array: CREATE TABLE csv.tutorials.contacts1 (Id INTEGER, Fname VARCHAR(50), Sname VARCHAR(50), Age INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE csv.tutorials.contacts2 (Fname VARCHAR(50), Sname VARCHAR(50), Age INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE csv.tutorials.contacts3 (CompanyName VARCHAR(50), CompanyID INTEGER, Fname VARCHAR(50), Sname VARCHAR(50), Age INTEGER);
SELECT COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY CompanyID ORDER BY Age) AS Company_Count FROM csv.tutorials.contacts3 WHERE bif:st_within(CompanyID, 12) > 0;
SELECT Fname, Age, bif:st_within(Age, 50) AS Age_Within FROM csv.tutorials.contacts2 WHERE Fname IS NOT NULL;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM csv.tutorials.contacts1 WHERE Age >= (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM csv.tutorials.contacts1) HAVING bif:st_within(Age, 50) = TRUE) AS FilteredContacts;
SELECT c1.Fname, c1.Age, c2.Sname FROM csv.tutorials.contacts1 c1 FULL OUTER JOIN csv.tutorials.contacts2 c2 ON c1.Age = c2.Age WHERE c1.Fname IS NOT NULL AND c2.Sname IS NOT NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT C.CompanyName, C.Fname FROM csv.tutorials.contacts3 C WHERE C.Age > ALL (SELECT Age FROM csv.tutorials.contacts1 WHERE Sname LIKE 'Smith%') AND bif:st_within(CompanyID, 12) = TRUE;
